* docs: fix SPARQL.md to accurately reflect implemented features

Update feature status matrix to match actual translator implementation:
- CONSTRUCT/DESCRIBE: mark as not supported
- Property paths: mark as 🚧 (parsed but simplified)
- GROUP BY/HAVING: mark as 🚧 (not fully translated)
- Aggregates: mark as 🚧 (not translated to Cypher)
- Date/Time, Hash functions: mark as not implemented
- Term constructors (IRI, BNODE, etc.): mark as not implemented
- String functions (STRBEFORE, STRAFTER, etc.): mark as not implemented
- Subqueries: mark as 🚧 (flattened, not nested)
- Named Graphs: mark as 🚧 (pattern traversed, no scoping)
- Remove misleading examples for unimplemented features
- Update intro to say 'subset of SPARQL 1.1'

commit.message:
Add TextRenderer class and enhance text rendering features (#18)

* Add TextRenderer class for rasterizing text and corresponding tests

- Implemented TextRenderer class to create RGBA textures from text strings using BitmapFont data.
- Added unit tests for BitmapFont and TextRenderer to ensure correct functionality and dimensions.
- Included tests for various scenarios including empty strings, single and multi-character strings, and different font sizes and styles.
- Updated CMakeLists.txt to include the new test file for TextRenderer.

* Add smoke test registration instructions and configuration for tools and examples

* Enhance text rendering implementation plan with new demo examples and acceptance criteria

* Add Pixel Arcade demo with text rendering and smoke test configuration

* Refactor code structure for improved readability and maintainability

* Add text metrics demo and enhance texture management in entities

- Introduced a new text metrics demo with associated smoke test scripts.
- Updated `Entity.h` and `Scene.h` to improve texture management, ensuring old textures are retired safely when replaced.
- Implemented two-stage resource retirement in `Scene` to manage GPU resource lifetimes more effectively.
- Enhanced descriptor set caching in `Entity.cpp` to prevent leaks and improve performance when textures are swapped.
- Added unit tests to verify that old textures remain alive until they are no longer needed after a swap.

* Fix TrueTypeFont safety, TTF alpha blending, stale metrics, and missing headers (#19)

* Initial plan

* Address all PR review comments: safety fixes, alpha blending, docs, and includes

Co-authored-by: derekdk <3893574+derekdk@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden TrueType font diagnostics and font name filtering

Co-authored-by: Derek Kowaluk <derekdk@me.com>

commit.message:
Add test_hw_watchdog_remaining_time to validate timeout range (#22873)

Adds a new test case that verifies the hardware watchdog remaining time
falls within a reasonable range (30-300 seconds). This catches platform
misconfigurations where the watchdog timeout is set absurdly short
(risking premature reboots) or too long (ineffective watchdog protection).

The test parses the 'Time remaining: N seconds' output from
'watchdogutil status' and asserts the value is within bounds. Platforms
where watchdogutil is not supported or the watchdog is unarmed are
gracefully skipped.

Fixes: https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/issues/22491

Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: analyze and optimize utility functions in src/plugins/utils (#8081)

* Initial plan

* docs: add performance analysis table for src/plugins/utils functions

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: fix consistency in usage counts across performance table

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: address code review feedback on PERFORMANCE.md consistency

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: optimize HIGH-classified utility functions with benchmarks

- overwriteGetterForCaching: value descriptor instead of getter (~37% faster reads)
- deepEqual: merge two loops into single pass (~17% faster for unequal objects)
- getFromMapOrThrow: === undefined instead of typeof (~6% faster)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: optimize CRITICAL-classified utility functions with benchmarks

- flatClone: spread { ...obj } instead of Object.assign (~319% faster)
- getProperty: fast path for dot-notation avoids getPathSegments (~281% faster)
- objectPathMonad: unrolled 2-segment fast path (~332% faster)
- getFromMapOrCreate: === undefined instead of typeof (~6% faster)
- deepClone: collapsed null check, moved Blob after Array check

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add test to verify pmon grep does not cause kernel panic (#22874)

* Add test to verify pmon grep does not cause kernel panic

Adds test_pmon_grep_no_kernel_panic which runs 'grep -r' inside the
pmon container over /sys/kernel/debug and then checks dmesg for any
new critical kernel messages (panic, Oops, Call Trace, BUG, RIP).

On some platforms (e.g. Cisco 8800 chassis), this operation previously
triggered kernel panics. The fix is to ensure the debug filesystem
contents are not accessible inside the pmon container.

Fixes: https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/issues/18394

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>

* Address review feedback: pmon pre-check, dmesg timestamp, grep string

- Add is_container_running pre-check so test fails explicitly if pmon is down
- Replace placeholder grep string with intentional dummy + explanatory comment
- Switch from line-count dmesg baseline to timestamp-based --since filtering
- Add POST_GREP_SETTLE_SECS delay before dmesg check for kernel log flush

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Coords accessor and improve ScanOutput/RunOutput API (#236)

* Add Coords accessor and improve ScanOutput/RunOutput API

- Add Coords(Mapping) class in range.py for xarray-like axis access:
  - coords['a'] and coords.a both return MRange/EList/Singular
  - __repr__ shows each axis name, type, and range/len summary
- Fix _make_scanned_parameter to handle bracket-string notation
  e.g. '[100,200,1000]' now correctly parses to EList([100,200,1000])
- ScanOutput gains:
  - axes: dict[str, Any] field (normalized ParameterValues per parameter)
  - coords property → Coords(self.axes)
  - shape property → (len_a, len_b,...) for grid; (n,) for zip
  - ndim property → len(axes) for grid; 1 for zip
  - __len__ and __iter__ delegating to points
- RunOutput gains coords property wrapping parameters as Singular values
  for a consistent interface with ScanOutput.coords
- simulation.scan() now returns ScanOutput instead of bare list;
  return type annotation updated; docstring updated
- Export Coords, RunOutput, ScanOutput, MRange, EList, Singular from
  run/__init__.py
- Update test_simulation_api.py to use new RunOutput/ScanOutput API
  instead of tuple unpacking
- Add tests/test_run_output.py: 29 tests covering Coords, bracket
  parsing, ScanOutput.coords/shape/ndim, RunOutput.coords, exports

Refactor DatFile classes to use scipp; add RunOutput/ScanOutput monitor access

- Replace numpy array storage in DatFile0D/1D/2D with scipp Dataset as
  primary representation: I with variances=I_err², N as named variables,
  bin positions as labelled scipp coordinates with physical units
- Add src/mccode_antlr/loader/_units.py: McCode → scipp unit mapping
  (AA→angstrom, AA^-1→1/angstrom, \gms/microseconds→us, etc.)
- Backward-compat .data and .variables properties preserved on DatFileCommon
- __add__ now uses scipp arithmetic for correct error-in-quadrature propagation
- Add RunOutput.dats property and __getitem__ passthrough to SimulationOutput
- Add ScanOutput.__getitem__(str) dispatching to _concat_monitor, which
  uses sc.concat + sc.fold to build a labelled Dataset across scan points
- Fix simulation.scan() to wrap raw results as RunOutput objects with
  per-point parameter dicts; handle empty-parameter edge case
- Update test_simulation_api.py to use new RunOutput API instead of
  old (result, SimulationOutput) tuple-unpacking
- Add tests/test_datfile_scipp.py: 33 tests covering DatFile0D/1D/2D
  structure, coords, units, backward compat, __add__, and unit mapping
- Add scipp as [scipp] optional extra in pyproject.toml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Fix] the DatFile.data property previously held a 3xN array; but should be deprecated

* [Fix] deprecated from typing_extensions for pre-3.13 Python

commit.message:
Fix O(2^N) hash blowup for chained relative component instances (#235)

* Fix O(2^N) hash blowup for chained relative component instances

Two bugs caused exponential hash cost:

1. Instance.__hash__ included at_relative[1] and rotate_relative[1] as
   full Instance object references. When both fields point to the same
   predecessor (AT ... RELATIVE PREVIOUS ROTATED ... RELATIVE PREVIOUS),
   Python's hash(tuple) calls hash() on each element with no caching,
   doubling the work at every level in the chain. Fix: replace references
   with their .name strings via a local _ref_id() helper.

2. Instance.__hash__ also included self.orientation (an Orient), whose
   position vector Expr grows exponentially due to symbolic expansion of
   composed rotations (cos(a+b) = cos²a - sin²b + ...). The orientation
   is a derived field fully determined by the position/rotation vectors and
   the predecessor chain, so excluding it from the hash is both safe and
   correct.

Secondary improvement: Comp.__hash__ changed from hash(repr(self)) to
hash(self.name). The repr is ~58x slower than the name hash.

Net result: hash cost is now O(1) per Instance regardless of chain depth.
Test test_chained_hashes_are_independent confirms flat timing across a
20-component chained instrument (the ILL_H142 guide section extract).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [Fix] Drop statistical time check, Fail fast

If a CI runner is running other tests simultaneously (likely),
it may fail the statistical check for successive hashing times due to
context switching.

This commit changes the test to instead verify that the total hashing
time does not exceed 5 seconds to hash all components four times; and if
a regresion is introduced the failure will occur after 5 seconds instead
of, e.g., the runner's maximum time cutoff parameter.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace @AndroidEntryPoint with EntryPointAccessors for BroadcastReceiver

@AndroidEntryPoint generates a Hilt wrapper that requires a full
Android component hierarchy, causing IllegalStateException in JVM
unit tests. Switch to manual EntryPointAccessors lookup which is
the recommended Hilt pattern for BroadcastReceivers and allows
direct field injection in tests.

Fixes all 10 UndoTransactionBroadcastReceiverTest failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve invite flow and runtime configuration

- add a public invite landing page with accept actions and preview data
- centralize app/runtime URL, host, port, and database configuration

Use idiomatic Maven pom.xml

- Remove redundant <packaging>jar</packaging> (default)
- Remove empty <repositories> and <profiles> blocks
- Replace compiler plugin <source>/<target> with maven.compiler.release property

commit.message:
fix: add orgs migration for research_data/researched_at columns, fix delete org route

- Add ALTER TABLE migration in db.js to add research_data and researched_at
  columns to existing orgs tables (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skips recreation
  so older production DBs were missing these columns, causing 500 on research)
- Fix deleteOrg route: add try/catch with error logging, remove redundant
  decodeURIComponent (Express already decodes route params), null out org_id
  on prompts before deleting org to avoid orphaned references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: git pull --rebase before SHA push to avoid race condition

Two rapid pushes caused both CI runs to commit SHA simultaneously,
then the second push got rejected. Pull --rebase before pushing fixes it.

repository.name: authenticator-tauri
repository.description: A modern, cross-platform, open-source two-factor authentication app built with Tauri v2 and React.

commit.message:
fix: accept OTP secrets shorter than 128 bits

Services like Coolify generate 80-bit TOTP secrets which are valid
per RFC 4226 but were rejected by the strict 128-bit minimum
enforcement in totp-rs. Use unchecked variants (new_unchecked,
from_url_unchecked) to accept any valid base32 secret regardless
of length, matching behavior of Google Authenticator and other apps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[ecmp] Skip ECMP balance test on dualtor topologies (#22760)

Dualtor topologies use mux cables on downstream ports. The ECMP
balance test sends traffic from downstream PTF ports expecting
direct forwarding to upstream ports, but in dualtor the mux cable
layer prevents packets from reaching the DUT correctly.

This causes consistent failures:
- dualtor-aa: 'DUT forwarded 0 packets' (0% pass rate)
- dualtor-120: 'Test results identical with/without offset' (25% pass rate)
- All t0/t1 topologies pass at 100%.

Add a topo_name parameter to is_supported() in the platform handler
classes. BroadcomPlatformHandler.is_supported() now checks if the
topology name contains 'dualtor' and returns False, causing the test
to be skipped via pytest.skip() in ECMPHashManager.

Signed-off-by: Zhaohui Sun <zhaohuisun@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
